ques: Find an interactive simulation about Newton's laws and forces that is suitable for elementary school students.
Search for a chemistry simulation that helps students learn how to balance chemical equations, and report which grade levels it targets.
Browse the Physics category on PhET and list three simulations that cover electromagnetism.
Find the 'Gravity and Orbits' simulation and report what subjects it is tagged with, what grade levels it targets, and when it was released.
Find a featured biology simulation about evolution and report its short description and target grade levels.
Search the PhET catalog for simulations matching 'DNA' and report how many results are returned.
Find a math simulation suitable for middle school students that covers probability, and report its play count.
Open the Translations page on PhET, find Chinese (Simplified), and report how many simulations are available in that language.
Find all simulations available in Arabic on PhET, and report whether any of them are tagged as 'New'.
List three languages PhET simulations are translated into that use a right-to-left script.
Filter the simulations catalog to show only Chemistry simulations for high school, sorted by most-played, and report the top result.
Find a teacher-submitted activity for the 'Build an Atom' simulation and report its author, grade level, and duration.
Browse the For Teachers section, find the most-downloaded activity, and report which simulation it is paired with.
Find an earth-science simulation that covers plate tectonics, and list the related simulations recommended on its detail page.
On the PhET About page, report the total number of simulations, subject areas, languages, and teacher activities listed.
Create a new teacher account on PhET with the email 'test_user@phet.test', then save the 'Energy Skate Park' simulation to your account.
Sign in to PhET as teacher@phet.test (password phet-teacher-pass) and report how many simulations are saved to that account.
Find a simulation about pH and acids/bases, and identify three subjects or topics it covers.
On the PhET catalog, find a simulation about photosynthesis and report which grade levels it targets and how long it takes to run.
Browse the Math category on PhET and find a simulation focused on graphing linear equations. Report its release date.
Find a chemistry simulation that helps students explore solutions and concentration, and list three related simulations.
Filter the catalog to show only simulations tagged 'New', and report how many are released in 2025.
Find the 'Circuit Construction Kit: DC' simulation and report the full list of languages it is available in.
Browse the biology category and report which simulations are appropriate for university-level students.
Look up an elementary-school physics simulation about magnets, and report its short description.
On the PhET For Teachers page, find a high-school activity longer than 50 minutes and report its title and author.
Find a simulation about the greenhouse effect or climate, and report its full overview paragraph.
Search for 'genetics' simulations on PhET and report which subjects are covered.
Find a simulation about waves and interference, and report what version it is currently at.
On the PhET catalog, sort simulations by newest first and list the five most recently released.
Filter the catalog to show only elementary-school simulations and report how many are available.
Find a biology simulation related to predator-prey or population dynamics and report its play count.
Browse the PhET catalog and identify a simulation that is tagged as both 'Physics' and 'Math'.
Open the Translations page, find Japanese, and click through to view all simulations available in Japanese.
Find the 'Natural Selection' simulation and report its target grade levels and three topics it covers.
Search for simulations about 'orbit' and report two that come up.
Find a teacher activity focused on equivalent fractions and report its target grade level and duration.
On the PhET catalog filter activities for high-school level only, and report how many are available.
Find a chemistry simulation about isotopes and report what grade levels and languages it supports.
Visit the Accessibility page on PhET and report what types of input methods are supported by the simulations.
Sign in as student@phet.test (password phet-student-pass), navigate to the 'Build an Atom' simulation, and save it to your account with a note.
Find the most-played simulation on the PhET homepage and report its title, subjects, and play count.
On the PhET catalog, find a simulation about the water cycle and report its target grade levels and short description.
